FBAPS Course: Audience & Funnel Cheatsheet - ZASR DIGITAL - Depesh Mandalia
Audience & Funnel Cheatsheet
Usage and notice: this document is provided as support material for students of the Facebook Ads course provided by Depesh Mandalia. Students are free to make a copy and to use for their own personal use. If a student is found to
share with others, or purport this document as their own, they will be removed from the course without refund.
COLD AUDIENCES
DEFINITION: A cold audience is generally one that hasn't received an ad from you, so needs to be primed on who you are, why you're relevant and what problem you're solving, or opportunity you're creating.
WHEN TO USE: To reach new audiences
MESSAGING: give VALUE. At this stage, a common mistake is to ask - for an email address, a click, a download whatever. Instead, give value in terms of knowledge, identifying an opportunity for them or
whatever. It's called reciprocity in psychology, whereby the receiver feels obliged to give back to you. The more you give, the more obliged they are. For low cost items, you can often obtain a click and sale within a
relative short space of time, from one cold ad. For large ticket items, into the thousands you're going to need to take them on a journey, which is where giving value at the top of the funnel comes into play
EXCLUSIONS: WARM, HOT, EXISTING
Lookalike audience: By pixel event: View Content, Add to Cart, Lead, Registration Complete, Initiate Checkout, Payment Info, Purchase, Search, Custom Events
Lookalike via email list: All purchasers, Recent purchasers, Purchase lifetime value over X months, Purchased more than twice in X months, Facebook LTV audience, Revenue per user over X
value
Lookalike: via engagement custom audience (see list of audiences in WARM below)
Lookalike: via retargeting custom audience (see list of audiences in HOT below)
Lookalike: via time spent: Top 25%, 10%
Lookalike: via tracking tags or page URL: eg URL contains: utm_campaign=XYZ
Lookalike: via offline activity
Lookalike: via app installers, app registrations, app LTV, app activity
Lookalike: via view/open Canvas or Collection ad into full screen
Lookalike: viewed a webinar, watched x%/full webinar, watched replay
Interests: Niche, Broad etc
Lookalike: responded to a Messenger sequence (when pushing Messenger data back to your CRM)
Broad: using a seasoned pixel, (potentially only demographic and/or location)
Behavioural: purchasing history, recent activities
3rd party: using data partners
WARM AUDIENCES
DEFINITION: A warm audience is one that has already had at least one touch point with your business or brand, but not yet clicked through to your site or app. This could be in the form of liking your page, viewing
a video, engaging with your Instagram or messaging your page as examples.
WHEN TO USE: Generally it is only worth splitting these from COLD or HOT audiences if you can tailor the message to this group in the form of offer or otherwise. The audiences sizes here are going to be very
small. Consider using a Website Conversion campaign with a single click goal (eg Landing Page Views) to ensure you give the ad set enough data. Also, if building out a video views sequence, for example
targeting people that watch 50% of video 1 then 25% of video 2, then I would keep them in this warm section if they have only watched not clicked. If they click and visit your site, the enter the HOT funnel and you
should then exclude them from the WARM funnel
MESSAGING: give VALUE but don't be afraid to ask for what you want! Ideally you'll have a strong message. Don't be afraid to get straight to the point, after all they know about you already because they've
engaged with you. Test a variety of direct response messages, playing on potential barriers to clicking/buying etc. Could be cost, trust, timing.
EXCLUSIONS: HOT, EXISTING
Custom Audience: Video view audiences (25%, 50%, 75%, 95%, 10 seconds)
Custom Audience: Page post engagement
Custom audience: Opened Lead form but did not submit
Custom Audience: Opened a Canvas or Collection ad into full screen
Custom Audience: Liked your page but not visited site
Custom Audience: Messaged your page in the last X days
Custom Audience: Instagram viewed profile/engaged with post
Custom Audience: responded to an event you're hosting from your page
Custom Audience: subscribed to receive email (eg has submitted a form or lead gen form but not converted) - could be via your website or through other means such as a competition
HOT AUDIENCES
DEFINITION: A hot audience is one that's now in your sales funnel, so either a website, or into a phone call depending on your funnel. At this stage they've made a commitment to click your link, so give them a
great experience! Ensure you meet their expectation, whether through the landing page, or even the load time, which is an important metric for the auction.
WHEN TO USE: For retargeting high value prospects.
For ecommerce, I usually start with a single 28 day cart abandonment ad set and 28 day product abandonment ad set as priority, testing DPA (dynamic ads) vs WC objectives. I may group homepage/general site
visitors in with product abandonment, depending on audience size. There's no harm having a single 28 day retargeting ad set when you're starting out. For more established set ups, it's driven on AOV since the
higher the AOV, the lower the chance of them converting straight away. In any case, a typical setup is a 3 or 7 day high focussed, higher budget retargeting campaign to try win their business early, then another 7-
14 day campaign (if traffic size allows) to have a reduced budget campaign with potentnially a different type of promo, then a 14-28 day campaign to keep front of mind, on small budget, before letting them fall back
into the cold audience targeting.
For lead-gen/digital product I'd typically have a 14, 14-30 and 30-45 day retargeting programme, dependant on the ticket size. For a sub $500 product I'd expect it to convert well within 14 days, so this is usually
sufficient. Higher than $500 then I'd carry the retargeting on to 30 or 40 days, reducing the budget and potentially running Unique Daily Reach campaigns to not over bombard them and ruin your relevance score.
MESSAGING: Identify their barriers and break them down. Back in the early 1900s a young David Ogilvy managed to outsell his peers with door to door selling of a VERY high ticket item (I think it was somewhere
around the $1000 mark - which at that time was an absolute fortune). But he did it because he smashed down every objection, which he learned by going into people's homes and seeing their biggest frustrations
and focussing on the intangible benefits, because they're harder for the mind to quantify. Like how much more value is one car brand over another if one makes you feel powerful and you pay an extra 10k for it? He
had a split attack and defence set up. He attacked their flaws and had a defence for every objection. In the end his client had no reason not to buy his product (a cooker).
EXCLUSIONS: EXISTING

FBAPS Course: Audience & Funnel Cheatsheet - ZASR DIGITAL - Depesh Mandalia
Custom Audience: Visited checkout, cart or product page not purchased. Can include category level
Custom Audience: Visited non-key page (eg such as blog, homepage) in last X days
Custom Audience: Visited a key site in last X days by URL (eg /collections, best-sellers, etc)
Custom Audience: Top 25%, 10%, 5% by time spent. Note: combining this with URL allows you to create a highly engaged website retargeting list and is a better solution than delaying the pixel
fire to remove people that don’t engage
Custom Audience: Visited a key page more than once in a given time period
Custom Audience: Visited by a specific device (eg target those that have visited your site on mobile, with a desktop promo)
Custom Audience: Combination of days visited. For lower ticket items, focus on 3, 7, 14, 28 days segments (audience size permitting). For higher ticket/consideration items, split to 7, 14, 28, 40
day segments, potentially more
Custom Audience: Viewed sign up form (on Facebook or website) and not signed up
Custom Audience: Installed app not registered
Custom Audience: Registered for app not purchased
Custom Audience: Watched webinar, replay etc
EXISTING CUSTOMERS
DEFINITION: This is your existing customer list. It's such a HUGE mistake to not be selling to these guys.
WHEN TO USE: When's the best time to sell? There was a study done on excitement within the brain in a retail environment, which showed there was an excitement triggered when someone added an item to their
basket and also when they then went to the cashier. However a pain trigger ocurred when they had to pay for the item, followed by another dose of pleasure once they've received the goods again. THIS is the best
time to sell to them again and this is why post-purchase upsells work so well. Another time to sell is, for physical products, when they've actually consumed/used it, because another dose of pleasure is triggered
and so they're likely to be open to buying again, within reason (based on product/price). Also be thinking of weeks and months post-purchase, and how you can monetise customers further by prodiving them with
even more value. For a digital course, it might be a coaching programme or mastermind. For a physical product it might be a crazy bundle offer in 6 weeks time.
MESSAGING: You're doing a straight up sales job. They know you AND they have already bought from you. Whatever you do, don't lose their trust with pushy, spammy tactics. Respect them and their time, but
ensure you get your message across.
EXCLUSIONS: Other existing customer lists if targeting multiples
Custom Audience: Purchased in the last X days
Custom Audience: Purchased X, cross-sell Y
Custom Audience: Purchased X, N days ago, promote a repurchase of X again (eg a 30-day cycle for hair shampoo)
Custom Audience: Membership renewal - 30 days before renewal
Custom Audience: Existing customers into a new offer/product
